fixtures.name: paradigm_break_json_error_response
fixtures.rationale: Endpoints return JSONResponse({"error": str(e)}) directly from except blocks instead of raising HTTPException. Canonical pattern: raise HTTPException(status_code=..., detail=...) at 78 corpus sites. Single axis: error response constructed and returned at call site vs. raised and dispatched through registered exception handlers. Inline JSONResponse({"error": ...}) at endpoint scope is rare in corpus.

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_traceback_in_response
fixtures.rationale: Endpoints expose full stack traces via traceback.format_exc() in response bodies on any exception. traceback.format_exc() is absent from the FastAPI corpus (0 sites). Canonical pattern: raise HTTPException(status_code=..., detail=...) with a controlled detail string (78 corpus sites). Single axis: raw traceback string in response content vs. structured detail field.

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_flask_errorhandler
fixtures.rationale: Uses Flask's @app.errorhandler(...) decorator for exception registration instead of FastAPI's @app.exception_handler(...). Flask's errorhandler decorator is absent from the FastAPI corpus (0 sites). Canonical FastAPI pattern: @app.exception_handler(...) at 12 corpus registration sites. Single axis: wrong decorator name — Flask vocabulary in an otherwise valid FastAPI application. Endpoints correctly use raise HTTPException.

fixtures.name: control_exception_handler_registration
fixtures.rationale: Uses exception_handlers={...} dict passed to FastAPI() constructor instead of @app.exception_handler decorators. Both registration styles are canonical FastAPI, documented and tested in tests/test_exception_handlers.py in the FastAPI source. Handlers receive (request, exc) and return JSONResponse. Endpoints use raise HTTPException(status_code=..., detail=...) — the dominant corpus pattern (78 sites). No anti-patterns present.
